The Great Article About Widgets

Widgets are small components that do useful things. This article explains, in considerable and deliberately verbose detail, why widgets matter to modern software and how they have evolved over the past several decades of engineering practice, tooling, and design taste across many different teams and organisations worldwide.

In the second paragraph we continue the discussion with even more words so that the Mozilla Readability algorithm confidently identifies this block as the primary readable content of the document, rather than the surrounding boilerplate, navigation chrome, or the promotional advertisement that sits above it in the markup.
